Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ALPHA CUBED INVESTMENTS, LLC's Motion For Preliminary Injunction.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's amended motion for preliminary injunction is denied. To prevail on this motion, plaintiff must present competent evidence showing imminent and irreparable harm, a likelihood of prevailing on the merits, and that the balance of equities and harms weighs in plaintiff's favor. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 527(a) ["A preliminary injunction may be granted at any time before judgment upon a verified complaint, or upon affidavits if the complaint in the one case, or the affidavits in the other, show satisfactorily that sufficient grounds exist therefor."]; see People v. Pacific Land Research Co. (1977) 20 Cal.3d 10, 21 [to secure preliminary injunction, moving party is required to make showing "by evidence which would be admissible in open court"]; Korean Philadelphia Presbyterian Church v. California Presbytery (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 1069, 1084 ["An injunction cannot issue in a vacuum . . . . It must be supported by actual evidence . . . ."]; Whyte v. Schlage Lock Co. (2002) 101 Cal.App.4th 1443, 1449-1450, 1457-1458 [affirming denial of preliminary injunction where evidence did not establish defendant threatened to or actually misappropriated trade secrets].) Plaintiff fails to meet that burden. The declarations of plaintiff's employees (John Walsh, Todd Walsh, Jared Oren, Steven Ford, and Tammy Lee) recount what plaintiff's clients allegedly told them or other ACI employees, who in turn related those conversations to the declarants. (E.g., Walsh Decl. pars. 9-15; Oren Decl. pars. 2-9; Ford Decl. pars. 4-8; Lee Decl. pars. 2-3, 5.) Defendant's hearsay objections to these declarations are sustained. (See Evid. Code secs. 1200, 1201.) The Court also sustains defendant's objection to the declarations' exhibits (e.g., client e-mails) because those documents are also hearsay. The e-mails are not affidavits or declarations under penalty of perjury per Code of Civil Procedure sections 2009 and 2015.5.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
